description: Common causes and risk factors that lead to the development of diabetes.
details: Diabetes develops when the body cannot properly use or produce insulin.
Common causes include:
• Genetics or family history
• Being overweight or having excess body fat
• Lack of physical activity
• Unhealthy diet high in sugar and processed foods
• Insulin resistance (body not responding properly to insulin)
• Damage to insulin-producing cells in the pancreas
• Hormonal changes (such as during pregnancy in gestational diabetes)
Risk increases with age, poor lifestyle habits, and certain medical conditions.
Not all diabetes is preventable, but many cases (especially type 2) are linked to lifestyle factors.

section_title: How Diabetes Works in the Body
description: A simple explanation of how insulin and blood sugar work and what goes wrong in diabetes.
details: When you eat food, it is broken down into glucose, which enters the blood.
Insulin is a hormone made by the pancreas that helps move glucose from the blood into body cells.
The cells use glucose for energy.
In diabetes, one of two main problems happens:
• The body does not produce enough insulin
• The body cannot use insulin properly, which is called insulin resistance
Because of this, glucose stays in the blood instead of entering the cells.
This causes blood sugar to rise.
If blood sugar stays high for a long time, it can damage organs such as the eyes, kidneys, nerves, and heart.

description: A clear explanation of whether diabetes can be cured and how it is usually managed over time.
